mnemonics: Cut the Wire (Edge): The minimum number of 'wires' (edges) you need to cut to disconnect the network (graph) is the edge connectivity (λ(G)).
keyPoints: Edge connectivity (λ(G)) is the minimum number of edges whose removal disconnects the graph.
A graph is k-edge-connected if its edge connectivity is at least k.
The edge connectivity of a graph cannot exceed the minimum degree of any vertex (δ(G)), i.e., λ(G) ≤ δ(G).
commonMistakes: Confusing edge connectivity (λ(G)) with vertex connectivity (κ(G)); one is about edges, the other is about vertices.
Assuming a high minimum degree (δ(G)) always means high edge connectivity; a graph can have a high minimum degree but a low edge connectivity if it has a 'bottleneck' of edges.
